name: Punahou Malasadas (Doughnuts)
recipeIngredient: Sugar and a dash of nutmeg
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the yeast with 1 teaspoon sugar and add to warm water. Let stand for 5 minutes.
2. Sift the dry ingredients together (flour, sugar, melted butter).
3. Beat the eggs and milk separately, then stir them into the flour mixture until combined.
4. Allow the dough to rise until doubled in size.
5. Punch down the dough and let it rise again.
6. Heat oil in a deep fryer to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
7. Dip fingertips in the hot oil or softened butter, then pinch off golf ball-sized pieces of raised dough.
8. Drop the pieces into the heated oil and cook until golden brown on one side.
9. Turn the doughnuts over and fry until golden brown on the other side.
10. Drain on paper towels and roll in sugar mixed with a dash of nutmeg.
11. Serve immediately.
Note: This recipe doubles well.
